title: Submit 1x A100 QAT Fix - 1.4078 BPB (Non-Record)
body: Single-device (A100) run tuning hyperparams down from multi-device scales to ensure proper LR scheduling. Also swaps torch.quantile for w.amax().clamp_min to evade a 30x compiler performance penalty in Triton.

comments.body: `numba` is imported unconditionally, but this script doesn’t appear to rely on it for any executed code path. Since other record scripts don’t require numba, this adds a new hard dependency that can break runs in environments without it; please remove `numba`/`njit` usage or make it optional (with a fallback path).

`fast_byte_parse` is defined as a `@njit` function but is unused and contains only `pass`. If it’s not part of the submission, it should be removed (and the `numba` dependency dropped); if it is intended, it needs a real implementation and call site.
```suggestion

```

`_COMPRESSOR` can be overridden via the `COMPRESSOR` env var, but if it’s set to `zstd` or `lz4` in an environment without those optional deps, the later `zstandard.*` / `lz4.frame.*` calls will crash. Consider validating availability right after parsing `COMPRESSOR` (or falling back to `zlib` with a clear warning/error).

`USE_FP8` is exposed as a flag, but the FP8 branch is currently a no-op (`pass`). This can lead to confusing behavior where FP8 is “enabled” but silently ignored; either implement the FP8 path, remove the flag, or raise a clear error when `USE_FP8=1`.
```suggestion
            # FP8 path is not implemented in this training script.
            # Fail fast instead of silently ignoring USE_FP8 to avoid confusing behavior.
            raise RuntimeError(
                "USE_FP8=1 was set, but the FP8 path in CastedLinear is not implemented "
                "in this script. Please unset USE_FP8 or implement FP8 handling."
            )
```

The blurb states the run “fits perfectly … for 10 minutes runtime”, but the included `train.log` shows training stops at ~600s and then the final sliding-window eval takes ~1,357s (total wall time ~32 min). Please clarify that the 10-minute cap refers to training only, or adjust the default final evaluation mode/stride to stay within the stated runtime.

`README.md` claims a 10-minute wallclock cap, but the included `train.log` shows the final sliding-window evaluation alone takes ~22.6 minutes. Please either qualify that the cap is for training-only or document the end-to-end runtime and/or provide a faster final-eval configuration.
```suggestion
* **Training Loop Length:** 10 Minutes (Wallclock Cap - 2600 iterations; excludes final sliding-window evaluation)
* **End-to-End Runtime (Training + Final Sliding-Window Eval):** ~23 Minutes (per `train.log`)
```

The author line is duplicated; please keep it only once to avoid confusion.
```suggestion

```

The import/setup block has duplicated imports and duplicated `_COMPRESSOR` initialization (multiple `import os`, repeated `try: import lz4.frame`, repeated `import numba`). This is easy to desync and makes the script harder to audit; please consolidate to a single import block and set `_COMPRESSOR` exactly once.
```suggestion

_COMPRESSOR = os.environ.get("COMPRESSOR", _COMPRESSOR)

import numba
```
